# Photosynthesis

Photosynthesis converts light energy into chemical energy stored in glucose. It
occurs in the chloroplasts of plant cells. The green pigment chlorophyll absorbs
light, mostly red and blue wavelengths. Photosynthesis has two stages: the
light-dependent reactions, which produce ATP and NADPH, and the Calvin cycle,
which fixes carbon dioxide into sugar. The process releases oxygen as a
byproduct.
